content:
# elizaos/elizaos.github.io Weekly Report (Apr 19 - 25, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
The development focus for the week of April 19–25, 2026, centered on expanding the project's infrastructure to support broader contributor tracking. By integrating the `elizaos/cloud` repository into the pipeline configuration, the team has enhanced the automated ingestion and leaderboard capabilities of the ecosystem. Additionally, the project saw active engagement regarding future feature directions, specifically concerning agent commerce and data privacy.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Pipeline Infrastructure Enhancements
The primary technical achievement this week was the expansion of the project's automated tracking capabilities.
* **Pipeline Configuration Updates:** Through PR [#243](https://github.com/elizaos/elizaos.github.io/pull/243), the `elizaos/cloud` repository (tracking the `dev` branch) was added to the `PIPELINE_REPOS` configuration. This integration ensures that contributor activity within the cloud repository is now captured for leaderboard tracking, supporting the project's commitment to transparent open-source collaboration.

## 🐛 Issues & Triage

### Closed Issues
Two significant proposals were addressed and closed this week, reflecting the project's evolving roadmap:
* **Agent Commerce:** Issue [#6977](https://github.com/elizaos/elizaos.github.io/issues/6977) regarding Agent Commerce and Merxex integration was closed, marking the conclusion of the initial proposal phase for these features.
* **Data Privacy:** Issue [#7007](https://github.com/elizaos/elizaos.github.io/issues/7007), which requested the implementation of a PII (Personally Identifiable Information) sanitization layer prior to LLM processing, was closed, signaling progress in defining the project's security and privacy standards.

### New & Active Issues
There were no new issues opened during this reporting period, and no active issues were reported as ongoing, indicating a focus on stabilizing existing infrastructure and processing current proposals.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The community activity this week was characterized by high-level architectural and feature-based discourse. The closure of issues [#6977](https://github.com/elizaos/elizaos.github.io/issues/6977) and [#7007](https://github.com/elizaos/elizaos.github.io/issues/7007) suggests active participation in defining the future scope of ElizaOS, particularly in the domains of commerce and data security. The successful integration of the `elizaos/cloud` repository demonstrates a collaborative effort to unify tracking across the broader ElizaOS ecosystem.
